item.title: Top Drivers of Bike Sharing Demand in Seoul
item.description: How to maintain a reliable supply of bikes regardless of changes in demand? In this project I build a highly interpretable model to predict the demand for bikes, as well as understand the main attributes responsible for its fluctuation. Keywords: Python, Machine Learning, Supervised Learning, Regression, Pandas, Numpy, Scikit-Learn, matplotlib, seaborn, deployed with github-pages. Here you can see more about this project!

item.title: Financial Complaints
item.description: Insights on Financial Complaints In this project I performed a simple Exploratory Data Analysis. I was looking to provide insights for an imaginary company that handles customer complaints and offers its services to companies in the financial sector. Technically speaking, I used the Google BigQuery API to consume the data and deployed a simple interactive report with Streamlit and Heroku. Keywords: Python, EDA, SQL, Google BigQuery, Pandas, Numpy, matplotlib, Plotly, deployed with Streamlit and Heroku.

item.title: Analyzing Supermarket sales
item.description: Analyzing Supermarket sales In this project, I performed a simple Exploratory Data Analysis to understand the difference in sales between three branches of the same supermarket. I raised three hypotheses and performed a non-parametric Mann-Whitney test to confirm my hypothesis, as I was working on non-normal distributions. Keywords: Python, EDA, Ipynb, Pandas, Numpy, matplotlib, seaborn. Here you can see the full report and here the repository.

item.title: 1985 Selected Cars and Trucks Data Visualization
item.description: How to visualize data quickly and effectively? In this data visualization project I draw a very easy but effective data presentation, aiming to present the main characteristics of 1985 selected cars and trucks. Keywords: Data Visualization, Flourish, EDA. Here you can see more about this project!

item.title: How to use Scikit-learn Pipeline to train different models at once
item.description: How to use Scikit-learn Pipeline to train different models at once I spent some time understanding how to properly use Pipeline in Scikit-Learn but the most examples I bumped into and the path I followed was focused on using it to wrap up some data pre-processing and feature engineering. Even though it is handy, I always had been stuck in the process of short-listing promising models - I strongly recommend that you check out the ml project checklist proposed by Aurélien Géron -, having to run each model in different tries.

item.title: Applying Tidy Data from scratch to financial statements
item.description: Applying Tidy Data from scratch to financial statements Introduction It might be counter-intuitive, and financial practitioners can be shocked, but the best way to deal with a large amount of financial statements is pivoting them around the date. The aim here is to put you in touch with the Tidy Data framework. Applying it with Python to the Financial Statements of publicly traded Brazilian companies, I’ll show you why this approach saves time and effort and should be used to prepare data for analysis.
